title: Sizing Artifact
description: Written by the SIZING sub-phase of seeding (P1.5R). Records the project_size dial — the depth classification that every downstream phase reads to right-size its behavior. Sits at seed_spec/sizing.json. See docs/design/adaptive-depth-dial.md for the classification rules and per-tier pipeline shape.

schema_version.description: Version tag so downstream consumers can branch on artifact shape.

project_size.description: The depth dial. XS = utility/tool, S = small app, M = standard SaaS, L = complex SaaS, XL = platform/multi-product. See docs/design/adaptive-depth-dial.md §Dial values.

signals.description: The counts the classifier read from BRAINSTORM's `## Classifier Signals` block.

reasoning.description: One-line rationale explaining why the classifier picked this tier from the signals. Useful for the human override gate.

classifier_version.description: Version of the classifier rules — e.g. 'v1'. Bumps when the rules change so historical sizings can be replayed or re-classified.

decided_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp. When the classification was written.

decided_by.description: classifier = automatic from signals. human-override = the gate accepted a human correction.

human_override.description: If decided_by = human-override, records what the classifier would have picked + the human's reasoning.

grew_from.description: History of mid-project upgrades. Each entry records a tier change triggered by the loop discovering new scope. Shrinks are never permitted — the dial grows or stays put.

defaults.description: Tier-based defaults stamped on the artifact at classification time (P1.5R PR 6). Live consumers (rouge-loop budget resolution, dashboard) read these instead of looking up tier → default elsewhere. Older artifacts written before PR 6 won't have this block; consumers should fall back to src/launcher/tier-defaults.js getDefaults(project_size).

budget_cap_usd.description: Default budget cap for this tier. Overridden by state.budget_cap_usd (per-project human-set); falls back to rouge.config.json budget_cap_usd if missing.

suggested_cycles.description: Suggested cycle count for this tier. Advisory — no hard cap is enforced on cycle count today (would be a separate gate). Surfaced in the dashboard for operator visibility.
